data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The broader peer universe comprises 8 private companies operating in this space, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no public comparables exist in this nascent category. The company has raised $14.4M to date.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($14.4M) as a baseline value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for progression from Series A to subsequent rounds, this approach yields $28.8M. A peer-median analysis was not viable given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data points within the narrow Drones & Autonomous cohort—only five same-sector peers exist in the data, with too few meeting tighter filtration criteria.

The triangulated valuation range is $24M to $33M, centered at $29M. Given reliance on a single methodology rather than multiple convergent approaches, this range reflects elevated methodological uncertainty. Key sensitivities include actual future fundraising velocity (which would validate or invalidate the 2.0x assumption) and comparative performance of named peers as market reference points. A more robust analysis would require materialization of additional same-stage comparables or operating metrics (revenue, unit economics, deployment scale) to anchor to market fundamentals.
